questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Match the condition of column-I with corresponding number of real roots of $f(x)=0$ is column-II and number of points of non-derivability of $y=f(|x|)$ in column-III, where $f(x)=a x^2+b x+c$. \(\begin{array}{l|l|l} \text{Column-I} & \text{Column-II} & \text{Column-III} \\ \hline \text{(I) } a^2+b^2+c^2-a b-b c-c a \leq 0 & \text{(i) } 0 & \text{(P) } 0 \\ \text{(II) } a^2+b^2+c^2+a b+b c+c a \leq 0 & \text{(ii) } 1 & \text{(Q) } 1 \\ \text{(III) } 3\left(a^2+b^2+c^2+1\right) \leq 2(a+b+c+a b+b c+c a) & \text{(iii) } 2 & \text{(R) } 3 \\ \text{(IV) } a^2+b^2+c^2 \leq 2 a+6 b+4 c+14 & \text{(iv) } \infty & \text{(S) } 5 \end{array}\)
Question: Which of the following is incorrect combination?
questions.solution:
I) $\begin{array}{ll}\because & a^2+b^2+c^2-a b-b c-c a \leq 0 \\ \Rightarrow & (a-b)^2+(b-c)^2+(c-a)^2 \leq 0 \quad \Rightarrow a=b=c \\ \therefore & f(x)=a x^2+a x+a \\ \therefore & D < 0 \\ \therefore & \text { no real root } \\ \therefore & |f(|x|)| \text { is always derivable except one point. } \\ \therefore & \text { (I) (i) (Q) }\end{array}$
(II)
$\begin{array}{ll}
\because & a^2+b^2+c^2+a b+b c+c a \leq 0 \Rightarrow(a+b)^2+(b+c)^2+(c+a)^2 \leq 0 \\
\Rightarrow & a+b=0, b+c=0, c+a=0 \\
\therefore & a=b=c=0 \\
\therefore & f(x)=a x^2+b x+c \text { is an identify } \\
\therefore & f(x)=0 \text { has infinite roots } \\
\therefore & f(x) \text { is always derivable. } \\
\therefore & \text { (II) (iv) (P) }
\end{array}$
(III)
$\begin{aligned}
& \because \quad(a-1)^2+(b-1)^2+(c-1)^2+(a-b)^2+(b-c)^2+(c-a)^2 \leq 0 \\
& \therefore \quad a=b=c=1
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad f(x)=0$ has no real root and $|f(|x|)|$ is non-derivable at exactly one point.
$\therefore \quad($ III) $(\mathrm{i})(\mathrm{Q})$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { (IV) } \because \quad(a-1)^2+(b-3)^2+(c-2)^2 \leq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad a=1, b=3, c=2 \\
& \therefore \quad f(x)=x^2+3 x+2 \\
&
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad f(x)=0$ has two distinct real roots and $y=|f(|x|)|$ is non-derivable at exactly one point.
$\therefore \quad \text { (IV) (iii) (Q). }$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

&

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $g(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}{[f(x)],} & x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \cup\left(\frac{\pi}{2}, \pi\right) \\ 3, & x=\pi / 2\end{array}\right.$ where $[x]$ denotes the greatest integer function and $f(x)=\frac{2\left(\sin x-\sin ^n x\right)+\left|\sin x-\sin ^n x\right|}{2\left(\sin x-\sin ^n x\right)-\left|\sin x-\sin ^n x\right|}, n \in R-\{1\}$, then
options: $g(x)$ is continuous and differentiable at $x=\pi / 2$, when $0 \lt n \lt 1$
$g(x)$ is continuous and differentiable at $x=\pi / 2$, when $n\gt1$
$\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})$ is continuous but not differentiable at $\mathrm{x}=\pi / 2$, when $0 \lt \mathrm{n} \lt 1$
$\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})$ is neither continuous nor differentiable, at $\mathrm{x}=\pi / 2$, when $\mathrm{n}\gt1$
questions.solution:
For $0 \lt \mathrm{n} \lt 1, \sin \mathrm{x} \lt \sin ^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{x}$ and for $\mathrm{n}\gt1, \sin \mathrm{x}\gt\sin ^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{x}$ Now, for $0 \lt \mathrm{n} \lt 1$,
$f(x)=\frac{2\left(\sin x-\sin ^n x\right)-\left(\sin x-\sin ^n x\right)}{2\left(\sin x-\sin ^n x\right)+\left(\sin x-\sin ^n x\right)}=\frac{1}{3}$
and for ,
$f(x)=\frac{2\left(\sin x-\sin ^n x\right)+\left(\sin x-\sin ^n x\right)}{2\left(\sin x-\sin ^n x\right)-\left(\sin x-\sin ^n x\right)}=3$

For $n\gt1, g(x)=3, x \in(0, \pi)$
$\therefore \mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})$ is continuous and differentiable at $\mathrm{x}=\frac{\pi}{2}$, for $0 \lt \mathrm{n} \lt 1$,
$g(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}
{\left[\frac{1}{3}\right]=0,} & x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \cup\left(\frac{\pi}{2}, \pi\right) \\
3, & x=\frac{\pi}{2}
\end{array}\right.$
$\therefore \mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})$ is not continuous at $\mathrm{x}=\frac{\pi}{2}$, Hence, $\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})$ is also not differentiable at $\mathrm{x}=\frac{\pi}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)= \begin{cases}\alpha+\frac{\sin [x]}{x}, & x\gt0 \\ 2, & x=0 \\ \beta+\left[\frac{\sin x-x}{x^3}\right], & x \lt 0\end{cases}$ (where [.] denotes the greatest integer function) if $\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})$ is continuous at $x=0$, then $\beta$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f\left(0^{+}\right)=\alpha \text { as } \sin [x]=0 \text { as } x \rightarrow 0^{+} \\ & f(0)=\beta-1 \text { as } \frac{\sin x-x}{x^3}=-\frac{1}{6}+\frac{x}{4!}+\frac{x^2}{5 \cdot!}+\ldots \ldots . . \\ & f(0)=2 \Rightarrow \alpha=2 ; \beta=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Number of points of non-differentiability of the function
$\left.g(x)=\left\{x^2\right]\left\{\cos ^2 4 x\right\}+\left\{x^2\right\}\left|\cos ^2 4 x\right|+x^2 \sin ^2 4 x+\left|x^2\right| \mid \cos ^2 4 x\right]+\left\{x^2\right\}\left\{\cos ^2 4 x\right\} \text { in }(-50.50) \text {. (where }$
[.] denotes the greatest integer function and {.} denotes the fractional part of function), is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { On simplifying, }\left[x^2\right] \cos ^2 4 x+\left\{x^2\right\} \cos ^2 4 x+x^2 \sin 4 x \\ & =x^2 \cos ^2 4 x+x^2 \sin ^2 4 x \\ & =x^2, \text { which is always differentiable }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution:
$\sin ^{-1}\left\{\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{1}{x}+x\right)\right\}$ check $f\left(1^{-}\right)=\sin ^{-1} 1^{+}, f(1)=\sin ^{-1} 1, f\left(1^{+}\right)=\sin ^{-1} 1^{-}$
Hence function is discontinuous so non differentiable

questions.q:
Paragraph: Match the condition of column-I with corresponding number of real roots of $f(x)=0$ is column-II and number of points of non-derivability of $y=f(|x|)$ in column-III, where $f(x)=a x^2+b x+c$. \(\begin{array}{l|l|l} \text{Column-I} & \text{Column-II} & \text{Column-III} \\ \hline \text{(I) } a^2+b^2+c^2-a b-b c-c a \leq 0 & \text{(i) } 0 & \text{(P) } 0 \\ \text{(II) } a^2+b^2+c^2+a b+b c+c a \leq 0 & \text{(ii) } 1 & \text{(Q) } 1 \\ \text{(III) } 3\left(a^2+b^2+c^2+1\right) \leq 2(a+b+c+a b+b c+c a) & \text{(iii) } 2 & \text{(R) } 3 \\ \text{(IV) } a^2+b^2+c^2 \leq 2 a+6 b+4 c+14 & \text{(iv) } \infty & \text{(S) } 5 \end{array}\)
Question: Which of the following is correct combination?
questions.solution:
I) $\begin{array}{ll}\because & a^2+b^2+c^2-a b-b c-c a \leq 0 \\ \Rightarrow & (a-b)^2+(b-c)^2+(c-a)^2 \leq 0 \quad \Rightarrow a=b=c \\ \therefore & f(x)=a x^2+a x+a \\ \therefore & D < 0 \\ \therefore & \text { no real root } \\ \therefore & |f(|x|)| \text { is always derivable except one point. } \\ \therefore & \text { (I) (i) (Q) }\end{array}$
(II)
$\begin{array}{ll}
\because & a^2+b^2+c^2+a b+b c+c a \leq 0 \Rightarrow(a+b)^2+(b+c)^2+(c+a)^2 \leq 0 \\
\Rightarrow & a+b=0, b+c=0, c+a=0 \\
\therefore & a=b=c=0 \\
\therefore & f(x)=a x^2+b x+c \text { is an identify } \\
\therefore & f(x)=0 \text { has infinite roots } \\
\therefore & f(x) \text { is always derivable. } \\
\therefore & \text { (II) (iv) (P) }
\end{array}$
(III)
$\begin{aligned}
& \because \quad(a-1)^2+(b-1)^2+(c-1)^2+(a-b)^2+(b-c)^2+(c-a)^2 \leq 0 \\
& \therefore \quad a=b=c=1
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad f(x)=0$ has no real root and $|f(|x|)|$ is non-derivable at exactly one point.
$\therefore \quad($ III) $(\mathrm{i})(\mathrm{Q})$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { (IV) } \because \quad(a-1)^2+(b-3)^2+(c-2)^2 \leq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad a=1, b=3, c=2 \\
& \therefore \quad f(x)=x^2+3 x+2 \\
&
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad f(x)=0$ has two distinct real roots and $y=|f(|x|)|$ is non-derivable at exactly one point.
$\therefore \quad \text { (IV) (iii) (Q). }$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(\mathrm{x})=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}\frac{1}{|\mathrm{x}|}, & |\mathrm{x}| \geq 1 \\ \mathrm{ax}^2+\mathrm{b}, & |\mathrm{x}| \lt 1\end{array}\right.$ be continuous and differentiable everywhere. Then a and b are -
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& f(x)= \begin{cases}-\frac{1}{x}, & x \leq-1 \\
a x^2+b, & -1 \lt x \lt 1 \\
\frac{1}{x}, & x \geq 1\end{cases} \\
& f^{\prime}\left(1^{+}\right)=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{\frac{1}{1+h}-1}{h}=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{-h}{h(1+h)}=-1 \\
& f^{\prime}\left(1^{-}\right)=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{a(1+h)^2+b-1}{h} \\
& =\lim _{n \rightarrow 0} \frac{a+b-1+2 a h+h^2}{h} \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}=1\{\because \text { function differentiable at } \mathrm{x}=1\} \\
& f^{\prime}\left(1^{-}\right)=2 \mathrm{a} \\
& f^{\prime}\left(1^{-}\right)=\mathrm{f}^{\prime}\left(1^{+}\right) \Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{a}=-1 \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}=-\frac{1}{2} \\
& a+b=1 \Rightarrow b=\frac{3}{2} .
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
STATEMENT 1: Consider a function $f(z)=\frac{1}{1+z^2}$ where $z \in$ complex number then, $f(z)$ is continuous inside a unit circle centred at origin.
STATEMENT $2:|z| \neq 1$ if $f(z)$ is continuous.
options: Both the statements are true and Statement 2 is correct explanation of Statement 1.
Both the Statements are true and Statement 2 is not the correct explanation of Statement 1.
Statement 1 is true and Statement 2 is false.
Statement 1 is false and Statement 2 is true.
questions.solution:
$\frac{1}{1+z^2}$ is continuous every where except where $1+z^2=0$
$\Rightarrow z= \pm i$ so when $|z| \lt 1$ then above points are excluded so $f(z)$ is continuous
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The function $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{l}\frac{a\left|x^2-x-6\right|}{6+x-x^2}, x \lt 3 \\ b \lim _{x \rightarrow 3}\left(\frac{x \int_3^x t d t}{x-3}\right), x=3 \\ \frac{x-[x]}{x-3}, x\gt3\end{array}\right.$
is continuous at $x=3$. If $f(3) \neq 0$, then $\{a, b\}$ is (where [ ] denotes the greatest integer function)
questions.solution:
$f(x)$ is continuous at $x=3$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}\left(3^{-}\right)=\mathrm{f}(3)=\mathrm{f}\left(3^{+}\right)$
$\operatorname{Now} f\left(3^{-}\right)=\lim _{x \rightarrow 3^{-}} \frac{a\left|x^2-x-6\right|}{6+x-x^2}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 3^{-}} \frac{-a\left(x^2-x-6\right)}{6+x-x^2}=a$
$\begin{aligned} & f\left(3^{+}\right)=\lim _{x \rightarrow 3^{+}} \frac{x-[x]}{x-3}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 3^{+}} \frac{x-3}{x-3}=1 \\ & f(3)=b \lim _{x \rightarrow 3}\left(\frac{x \int_3^x t d t}{x-3}\right)\left(\frac{0}{0} \text { form }\right)=b \lim _{x \rightarrow 3}\left(\frac{x^2+\int_3^x t d t}{1}\right)=9 b\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow a=9 b=1 \Rightarrow b=\frac{1}{9}$ and $a=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A point $(x, y)$ where function $f(x)=[\sin [x]]$ in $(0,2 \pi)$ is not continuous, is (where [.] denotes greatest integer $\leq x$ )
questions.solution: In $(0,2 \pi)$ check for $1,2,3,4,5,6$ function is not continuous for 4 Hence, $(x, y)$ is $(4,-1)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
STATEMENT 1: $f(z)=\operatorname{Re}(z)$ is a function where $z \in$ complex number then $f(z)$ is continuous but not differentiable
STATEMENT 2: $\lim _{\substack{\Delta x \rightarrow 0 \\ \Delta y \rightarrow 0}} \frac{f(z+\Delta z)-f(z)}{\Delta z}=\lim _{\substack{\Delta y \rightarrow 0 \\ \Delta x \rightarrow 0}} \frac{f(z+\Delta z)-f(z)}{\Delta z}$ for any point $z$
options: Both the statements are true and Statement 2 is correct explanation of Statement 1.
Both the Statements are true and Statement 2 is not the correct explanation of Statement 1.
Statement 1 is true and Statement 2 is false.
Statement 1 is false and Statement 2 is true.
questions.solution: Continuity : for any point $z, \lim _{z \rightarrow z_0} f(z)=\lim _{z \rightarrow z_0} x=x_0=f\left(z_0\right)$. Non differentiable : for any point $z$, $f^{\prime}(z)=\lim _{\Delta z \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(z+\Delta z)-f(z)}{\Delta z}=\lim _{\Delta z \rightarrow 0} \frac{\Delta x}{\Delta x+i \Delta y}$ now $\lim _{\substack{\Delta x \rightarrow 0 \\ \Delta y \rightarrow 0}} \frac{\Delta x}{\Delta x+i \Delta y}=0$ and $\lim _{\substack{\Delta y \rightarrow 0 \\ \Delta x \rightarrow 0}} \frac{\Delta x}{\Delta x+i \Delta y}=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

options: area bounded by $f(x), x=1, y=0$ is $\frac{1}{3}$
